column1: # RustyNES Game Genie code-name database (v1.2.0 Workstream D, D3; categorized v1.8.9).
# A ROM-indexed list of well-known Game Genie codes drawn from the public
# Galoob code books and the community Game Genie code lists (the codes and the
# effect names are factual/functional descriptions, not Nintendo program code).
# RustyNES consumes this ENTIRELY in the frontend: the cheat panel shows the
# codes that match the loaded ROM as a category-grouped pick-list (and a
# code -> game reverse lookup); selecting one feeds the EXISTING
# Format (tab-separated; '#' lines and blank lines are comments):
# The Category column is OPTIONAL (rows that omit it default to "Misc"); it
# groups/filters effects in the UI (e.g. Lives, Items, Invincibility, Movement,
# Start, Weapons, Difficulty, Misc). CRC32 is the "ROM CRC" of PRG-ROM + CHR-ROM
# with the 16-byte iNES header (and any 512-byte trainer) excluded — the same key
# `crate::game_db::rom_crc32` computes, so a loaded ROM resolves its codes by CRC.
# Multiple rows may share a CRC (a game has several codes). Codes are validated
# through `GenieCode::new` before being offered, so a malformed row is silently
# This is a CURATED starter catalog of widely-documented codes, not an exhaustive
# dump — users extend it in-app via manual entry with a custom effect name +
# category (the cheat panel persists those alongside these). It is committed
# (CC0-style factual data; small) and compiled in via `include_str!`; keep it
# modest — it ships in the binary AND the wasm bundle. Commercial Nintendo ROMs
# are NEVER committed; only the codes are.
# The Legend of Zelda (USA) — PRG+CHR CRC 0xD7AE93DF
